summary: Ronacher announces his departure from Sentry after a decade, framing it as a hard but necessary transition to a new chapter. He reflects on how Sentry shaped his life and career, from early community ties to scaling a company far beyond his expectations. The post highlights pride in Sentry’s technical growth, open-source commitments, and the people who made it possible. Leaving is emotionally difficult because the company is intertwined with his personal milestones and identity. He concludes with a cautious but determined optimism about starting something new, driven by evolving motivations and a desire to build for a broader audience.
key_points: His relationship with Sentry began in the Django community and evolved into a decade-long role shaping the company’s growth.
Sentry surpassed early expectations, achieving major scale, a broad product suite, and significant organizational milestones.
Technical accomplishments included maintaining high-quality multi-platform SDKs and scaling ingestion to massive volume.
He emphasizes staying committed to open-source values and experimenting with licensing and dependency funding.
Leaving is emotionally difficult but fueled by a renewed drive to build something with wider impact.
money_quotes: Sentry has been more than just a job, it has been a defining part of my life.
While we may have stumbled at times, we've also learned immensely throughout this time.
Walking away from something you love is never easy and leaving Sentry is hard.
We stayed true to our Open Source principles, even when other options were available.
There's a measure of hubris in assuming lightning strikes twice, and as humbling as that realization is, it also makes me that much more determined.
Today, I stand in an open field, my backpack filled with experiences and a renewed sense of purpose.
I want to build something that speaks to more people, something that, hopefully, even my children will find meaningful.
key_insight: Leaving Sentry is a painful but purposeful step toward a new venture shaped by hard-earned experience, evolving values, and a desire for broader impact.
